description: "Best performer" is meaningful in several different frames (absolute return, risk-adjusted, longest active). Reviewers fail MECE and objective_source, blocking finalize.

input.question: Which ETF will be the best performer of 2027?

reviews.reviewProse: "Best performer" can mean absolute return, risk-adjusted return, or Sharpe ratio. Each of those can resolve to a different winner; without a single chosen metric the market is effectively three markets overlaid.

rubricVotes.rationale: Multiple metrics can select different winners so outcomes are not mutually exclusive.
Bloomberg Markets has no single canonical "best performer" ranking.
Each outcome conflates metric and rank.

criticisms.rationale: Pick exactly one metric and state its exact computation.
Cite a specific Bloomberg page or Morningstar rank, not a homepage.
